content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob are playing a tournament which consists of NNN matches.

Right now, they have completed MMM (1≤M≤N1 \le M \le N1≤M≤N) matches already, and you have the results from those matches. You are given a binary string SSS of length MMM, where Si=1S_i = 1Si​=1 means that Alice won the iii-th match and Si=0S_i = 0Si​=0 if Bob won the iii-th match.

At the end, whoever has won more matches wins the tournament. However, if both of them win equal number of matches, the tournament ends in a tie instead.

Given the matches that have already been played, is it still possible for the tournament to end in a tie? Print Yes\text{Yes}Yes if it is possible, and No\text{No}No otherwise.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and MMM, the number of total matches to play, and the matches already played.
The second line contains a binary string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line Yes\text{Yes}Yes if is it possible to still have a draw and No\text{No}No otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The tournament has already completed all the matches, and it was a 1−11 - 11−1 tie.
Test Case 2 : Alice has won 333 matches, there is no way for Bob to tie now.
